summary.intent: Fix two P2 PR review comments on a Rust GraphQL session key consistency fix: replace panicking assertions in mock methods and add a missing test for `_session_key` override precedence.
summary.outcome: Both issues resolved: mock methods in `graphql_chat_binding.rs` now return errors instead of panicking, and a new unit test `history_internal_session_key_overrides_public_session_key` was added to `crates/chat/src/lib.rs`. All tests pass and changes were committed.

repo: Axum handlers catch panics, so `assert_eq!` in mock async methods will swallow failures silently — mocks must return `Err(...)` instead.
`resolve_session_key_from_params` on `LiveChatService` implements a three-tier precedence: `_session_key` (internal override) > `sessionKey` (public param) > active connection session key.
The `_session_key` param is an internal runtime injection, not exposed through the GraphQL schema — tests for its precedence belong in `crates/chat/src/lib.rs`, not the GraphQL integration tests.
Existing chat tests use a `history` call as the simplest path to exercise `resolve_session_key_from_params` since it reads from the session store without needing full message dispatch setup.

code.finding: MockDispatch's `send`, `history`, and `active` methods used `assert_eq!` which panics inside async Axum handlers — replaced with conditional `Err(...)` returns for reliable test failures.

Added `history_internal_session_key_overrides_public_session_key` test alongside existing `history_prefers_public_session_key_over_connection_active_session` to cover the `_session_key` > `sessionKey` precedence case.

workflow: PR review comments from greptile are tagged with priority (P2) and fetched via `gh` CLI — both general comments and inline review comments need separate API calls.
For Rust projects, running `cargo fmt --check` after edits before committing catches formatting issues early.

friction: Locating the right test file for the precedence test required several rounds of grep — the `_session_key` param is not exposed via GraphQL so the obvious location (GraphQL integration tests) was wrong.
Multiple reads of `crates/chat/src/lib.rs` were needed to understand `LiveChatService` construction and find the existing test pattern to follow.
